name: Harissa Hot Chili Paste (Tunisia)
recipeIngredient: 3 ounces mild and hot chilies, dried
mixture of anchos, New Mexican, and guajillos
recipeInstructions:
1. Stem the seeds and break up the chilies. Place in a bowl and pour over boiling water.
2. Cover and let stand 30 minutes. Drain; wrap in cheesecloth and press out excess moisture.
3. Repeat for the red bell pepper.
4. Grind the chilies in a food processor with garlic spices, roasted red bell pepper, and salt.
5. Add enough olive oil to make a thick paste.
6. Pack the mixture into a small dry jar; cover the harissa with a thin layer of oil.
7. Close the jar tightly and keep refrigerated. Will keep 2 to 3 weeks in the refrigerator with a thin layer of oil.
Table Harissa Sauce:
Serve at the table as an accompaniment to meat or fish, to enhance the flavor of salads, or as an accompaniment to Tunisian couscous: Combine 4 teaspoons harissa paste, 4 teaspoons water, 2 teaspoons olive oil, and 1 or 2 teaspoons fresh lemon juice in a small bowl. Blend well to make 1/4 cup.
